summary: Phase 5 release: canonical source metadata, a visible in-app build identity, release-readiness checks, delivery documentation and final deployment guidance.
features: The Settings > About panel now renders the canonical runtime version and build identifier, so the app itself exposes the exact release currently loaded by the browser.
Release metadata is synchronized across the package, PWA manifest, release descriptor, service worker, health endpoint, local asset manifest and project manifest.
A dedicated Phase 5 verifier checks that release documentation, in-app release identity, Kurdish/English copy and deployment contracts remain aligned before the static build runs.
The final release guide documents the local-first runtime, protected project recovery, safe exports, controlled updates and Termux/Vercel delivery sequence.

buildPolicy.fallback: npm run model:sync remains recovery-only; production clients use the verified local asset.
buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: no remote MediaPipe module or model is loaded by the deployed browser; Phase 3 tracking and continuity run locally.
buildPolicy.projectSafety: Recovery journaling is session-scoped and bounded. It never uploads drawing data and is cleared after a verified local persistence commit.
buildPolicy.export: Exports save the current project first. Unsupported WebP encoding falls back to a PNG file locally.
buildPolicy.updates: Applying a release requires a completed local save before service-worker promotion or cache-busted navigation.
buildPolicy.releaseReadiness: Phase 5 validates canonical release identity in source, UI, documentation and generated deployment assets before the release is marked ready.
buildPolicy.delivery: Use npm run vercel:build locally or in CI. The deployment uses self-hosted MediaPipe runtime and the verified same-origin hand model only.
